title: erigeron peregrinus var. thompsonii
intro: Erigeron peregrinus var. thompsonii, also known as Thompson's fleabane, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its white or pink flowers and hairy leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Erigeron peregrinus var. thompsonii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in salves and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
